symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky lower abdominal pain with explosive watery diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky secretory diarrhoea fits a microscopic colitis flare.
Continued flatus/stool and absence of vomiting/distension argue strongly against mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colicky pain easing after defecation; afebrile; no RLQ/blood

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever/RLQ localisation lowers appendicitis probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-rich-meal onset on known microscopic colitis (normal colonoscopy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dietary trigger on known microscopic colitis supports a flare.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination if pain persists/worsens
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for peritonism the abdomen cannot be examined for remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Budesonide trial via GP if refractory
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Maintenance option for microscopic colitis after review.
